text: All of the rumours around Spurs' transfers this season (and Senesi's signing) is just further evidence that the big 6 doesn't depend on position - it's reputation & finance based. text: Big 6 became a financial bracket after the 2010s where those teams consistently occupied the European places and challenged for honors. Their success coincided with the 2nd explosion in popularity for the Prem with the advent of streaming services and big TV deals in North America. Spurs rise as a global footballing power is probably the perfect example of that.

notes: prelabeled:reviewed - claim + specific causal reasoning (TV deals/streaming timing) + named example

text: I mean yeah it's just about revenue. Revenue barely changes season to season even. It's why when some like Chelsea, United and Spurs finish in the bottom half they can still spend £200m to get themselves out of it.

text: As with any area of life you need money to make money, and the clubs with the money are the ones that make the money, and the rules then allow them to spend the money and subsequently make more money. Year on year performance is irrelevant to this. In previous centuries clubs could catch up with external investment but that option is no longer available.
